title: Local vs Cloud Execution
summary: Synthetic planning packet for a product decision on local reliability, hosted convenience, and demo risk.

sources.title: Reliability review
sources.body:
Local operation keeps the demo inspectable and makes file-backed artifacts easy to explain. The tradeoff is setup fragility: dependency pinning, seed commands, and deterministic fixture validation must be boring before a live walkthrough.

Briefing relevance: This document is part of the authored synthetic source packet for the runtime decision case. The strongest signal for the requested briefing is: Local operation improves inspectability but requires reliable dependency pinning and seed data. A secondary signal, which may or may not belong in the final recommendation depending on the user request, is: Hosted previews reduce onboarding friction but are outside the current hosting plan.

Decision boundary: A good briefing should cite this document only for claims it directly supports. The main trap to avoid is: Do not invent a production hosting plan. If this document is cited for a broader rollout, approval, or safety claim, the evaluator should check whether the body text actually contains that gate.

sources.title: Go-to-market note
sources.body:
Hosted previews reduce onboarding friction for reviewers who do not want to run the app locally. The current roadmap, however, has no production hosting plan and no owner for preview secrets, deploys, or data retention.

Briefing relevance: This document is part of the authored synthetic source packet for the runtime decision case. The strongest signal for the requested briefing is: Hosted previews reduce onboarding friction but are outside the current hosting plan. A secondary signal, which may or may not belong in the final recommendation depending on the user request, is: Public fixtures must exclude private paths, customer identifiers, and unreleased vendor data.

Decision boundary: A good briefing should cite this document only for claims it directly supports. The main trap to avoid is: Do not treat public fixture safety as optional. If this document is cited for a broader rollout, approval, or safety claim, the evaluator should check whether the body text actually contains that gate.

sources.title: Security note
sources.body:
Public fixtures must avoid private planning paths, customer identifiers, and unreleased vendor data. Hosted previews would also need environment-variable review before anyone shares a link externally.

Briefing relevance: This document is part of the authored synthetic source packet for the runtime decision case. The strongest signal for the requested briefing is: Public fixtures must exclude private paths, customer identifiers, and unreleased vendor data. A secondary signal, which may or may not belong in the final recommendation depending on the user request, is: Runtime pinning reduced local demo risk in rehearsal.

Decision boundary: A good briefing should cite this document only for claims it directly supports. The main trap to avoid is: Do not expose holdout tuning labels in the product UI. If this document is cited for a broader rollout, approval, or safety claim, the evaluator should check whether the body text actually contains that gate.

sources.title: Demo rehearsal
sources.body:
The local demo failed once because a stale runtime used the wrong package manager. After mise pinned the runtime, the second rehearsal succeeded and reviewers valued seeing artifacts written directly into the repo.

Briefing relevance: This document is part of the authored synthetic source packet for the runtime decision case. The strongest signal for the requested briefing is: Runtime pinning reduced local demo risk in rehearsal. A secondary signal, which may or may not belong in the final recommendation depending on the user request, is: Local operation improves inspectability but requires reliable dependency pinning and seed data.

Decision boundary: A good briefing should cite this document only for claims it directly supports. The main trap to avoid is: Do not invent a production hosting plan. If this document is cited for a broader rollout, approval, or safety claim, the evaluator should check whether the body text actually contains that gate.

sources.title: Reviewer calibration document
sources.body:
Reviewers use this calibration document to decide whether the briefing selected the right evidence from the packet. The expected coverage points for this case are: Local operation improves inspectability but requires reliable dependency pinning and seed data. Hosted previews reduce onboarding friction but are outside the current hosting plan. Public fixtures must exclude private paths, customer identifiers, and unreleased vendor data. Runtime pinning reduced local demo risk in rehearsal.

The calibration note is intentionally not a model answer. It gives the evaluator enough context to distinguish direct support from plausible synthesis. A strong briefing should still rely on the source-specific operational notes above when making claims, and it should use this document only to understand how the packet will be judged.

The repeated failure modes under review are runtime-choice, public-data-safety. When a generated briefing cites sources broadly without connecting the claim to a concrete caveat, gate, or decision boundary, reviewers should mark the citation as weak even if the source is topically related.

sources.title: Risk and distractor document
sources.body:
This document records traps that make the packet realistic for eval work. Known traps include: Do not invent a production hosting plan. Do not treat public fixture safety as optional. Do not expose holdout tuning labels in the product UI.

Several details in the packet are deliberately adjacent to the user request but not sufficient for a shipping recommendation. For example, a metric improvement should not automatically become a rollout recommendation, a faster path should not automatically become the default path, and a draft or generated recommendation should not be treated as an approved action.

The intended task is: Advise whether the demo should stay local or move to hosted previews for the next iteration. A good answer should prioritize the evidence that changes the recommendation, identify caveats that block overconfident claims, and leave open questions where the packet does not contain enough information.
